message:
bench(patterns): dedicated PatternEngine::quick_reject Criterion bench

## Why

PatternEngine::quick_reject is the cheap public prefilter on the hot
path before the heavier detect() flow kicks in. A regression in the
memchr + Bloom gate behind quick_reject is the kind of thing that is
easy to miss — detect() benches cover the *combined* cost, so a
slow-down isolated to quick_reject can hide under the noise of the
downstream work.

This adds a dedicated bench group so we track quick_reject by itself
and alert cleanly if it drifts.

## What the bench covers

  - Two payload shapes: pure no-match (engine should short-circuit
    fast) and near-miss text that trips the Bloom filter without
    actually matching a rule (engine still rejects, but pays a
    little more cost).
  - Four sizes across 4 orders of magnitude (1 KiB, 16 KiB, 64 KiB,
    1 MiB) so we can see how the prefilter scales.
  - Wraps inputs in `std::hint::black_box` so the optimizer can't
    elide the call.
  - Declares the budget ("median < 5ms across no-match and near-miss
    corpora") via the shared BenchBudget / CiBudgetEntry mechanism so
    CI's perf guardrails pick the new group up automatically.

## bench_common.rs

Adds a matching CiBudgetEntry for the `patterns_quick_reject/`
group-prefix so CI's post-bench budget check enforces the 5 ms
ceiling. Same ceiling as the existing `pattern_detection/` entry, so
the combined detect cost stays comparable to the
quick_reject-isolated cost.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
